text.title: ai-assisted-specification-writing Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for ai-assisted-specification-writing as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Providing vague 1-2 sentence feature descriptions to the LLM
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Specs generated from vague intent reliably contain hallucinated requirements that seem plausible but conflict with actual product intent. The LLM fills gaps with the most common interpretation, which may not match yours.

failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: LLMs generate requirements for implied topics. A payment spec without "this spec does not cover refunds or chargebacks" will contain refund and chargeback FRs that were never intended and bloat implementation scope.

failure-mode.description: Relying on AI for domain-specific business rules
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: AI lacks your specific organizational context, pricing model, regulatory constraints, and product history. Business rules stated implicitly in prompts are misinterpreted; all domain rules must be stated explicitly.

failure-mode.description: Accepting AI-generated spec without reviewing every FR
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: AI generates requirements that are technically coherent but businesslogically incorrect. Reviewing only structure and format misses the plausible-sounding-but-wrong requirements that cause incorrect implementation.

failure-mode.description: Treating AI-generated Given-When-Then criteria as QA-ready without review
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Agent-generated acceptance criteria frequently test the implementation path rather than the requirement. Criteria must be behavior-focused: "when user submits invalid email, then error message appears" not "when validator.validate() throws, then catch block runs".

failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: AI cannot self-assess consistency within a single generation pass. A separate review call with explicit conflict-detection instructions catches contradictions between requirements that are invisible to the original drafting call.

failure-mode.description: Combining spec generation and implementation planning in one prompt
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Mixing both tasks in one prompt degrades quality for both outputs. The planning phase requires the spec to be complete and approved; generating both simultaneously skips the human review gate.

failure-mode.description: Generating a spec and immediately proceeding to implementation without human approval
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: This is the primary cause of wasted implementation work. The agent cannot self-assess whether a spec is good enough for implementation to start — that judgment requires a human with domain context.

failure-mode.description: Creating specs over 15,000 tokens in a single file
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: LLMs lose coherence in very long specs even within large context windows — they begin contradicting earlier requirements and losing track of stated constraints. Break large systems into 5-6 focused phases of 30-50 requirements each.
